channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: China threatens countermeasures after Trump threatens additional 50% tariff
item.description: China threatened Tuesday to take countermeasures against the United States "to safeguard its own rights and interests” in response to President Donald Trump’s threat of an additional 50% tariff on Chinese imports.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Swollen rivers are flooding towns in the US South after a prolonged deluge of rain
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Days of unrelenting downpours swelled rivers to near record levels across Kentucky on Monday, submerging neighborhoods and threatening a famed bourbon distillery in the state capital.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Catholic bishops end refugee aid partnerships with US government, citing funding cuts
item.description: The U.S. Conference of Catholic Bishops announced Monday that it is ending a half-century of partnerships with the federal government to serve refugees and migrant children, saying the “heartbreaking” decision follows the Trump administration’s abrupt halt to funding.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Colleges around the US say some international students' visas are being revoked
item.description: Colleges around the country are reporting cases of international students suddenly learning their visas have been revoked.
item.dc:creator: Annie Ma, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Carolina's highest court refuses to stop second firing squad execution
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina's highest court on Monday rejected the last major appeal from Mikal Mahdi, who is scheduled to die by firing squad later this week for the ambush killing of an off-duty police officer.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Maine sues the Trump administration over funding freeze after dispute over transgender athletes
item.description: SCARBOROUGH, Maine (AP) — Maine officials sued the administration of President Donald Trump on Monday to try to stop the government from freezing federal money in the wake of a dispute over transgender athletes in sports.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump honors Dodgers' 2024 World Series championship team
item.description: The 2024 World Series Champions, the Los Angeles Dodgers, were honored by President Donald Trump at the White House on Monday before opening a series with the Washington Nationals.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: Judge dismisses former Mississippi governor's defamation lawsuit against news outlet
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — A judge has dismissed a lawsuit by former Mississippi Gov. Phil Bryant that claimed a local news outlet defamed him in public comments about its Pulitzer Prize-winning reporting on the misspending of $77 million in federal welfare funds.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Idaho mom who killed 2 of her kids goes on trial over husband's death
item.description: Lori Vallow Daybell, the Idaho mother with doomsday religious beliefs who was convicted of killing her two youngest children and conspiring to murder a romantic rival, is on trial again. This time, she's accused in Arizona of conspiring to murder her estranged husband.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Distressed kitten rescued, adopted by woman who called for help
item.description: But after crews rescued the feline, named "Flush," from a storm drain, the woman fell in love with him and decided to adopt him.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: Man claims to be ICE agent, threatens to deport victims, deputies say
item.description: When deputies tracked the suspect down, he turned the story on the victims, claiming he was threatened with deportation.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: US stocks dip after careening through a manic day following Trump's latest tariff threat
item.description: U.S. stocks swung wildly in a manic Monday after President Donald Trump doubled down on his tariffs, despite seeing how much Wall Street wants him to do the opposite.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FAFSA has struggled for years. Then Trump cut the Education Department in half
item.description: Families and students are growing nervous about the fate of the Free Application for Federal Student Aid amid President Donald Trump's planned cuts to the Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Mega Millions tickets rise to $5, but the lottery promises more giant jackpots
item.description: Mega Millions players will get slightly better odds and should start seeing more billion-dollar jackpots, but at a cost — literally — with tickets for the multistate lottery more than doubling in price from $2 to $5.
item.dc:creator: Scott Mcfetridge, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump meets Netanyahu as first trade talks with world leaders begin and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he's not backing down, calls tariffs 'medicine' as markets reel
item.description: Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump said he didn't want global markets to fall, but also that he wasn't concerned about the massive sell-off either.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: George M. Johnson's 'All Boys Aren't Blue' tops 2024 list of most 'challenged' US library books
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Removing books from library shelves is no longer just a story of objections from a local community or an individual parent, the American Library Association says.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he hopes Sununu runs for Senate in New Hampshire
item.description: "He came to my office, came to the Oval Office, and met with Chris Sununu, and I support him fully. I hope he runs," Trump told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: RFK Jr. visits epicenter of Texas measles outbreak after death of second child who was infected
item.description: U.S. Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. visited the epicenter of Texas' still-growing measles outbreak on Sunday, the same day a funeral was held for a second young child who was not vaccinated and died from a measles-related illness.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI and AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: FAFSA has struggled for years. Then Trump cut the Education Department in half
item.description: Last year’s FAFSA cycle turned into chaos as the Biden administration attempted to simplify the forms.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson strikes deal with Rep. Luna on parental proxy voting
item.description: This marks a development that is set to unlock the House floor after the explosive issue — and the GOP rebellions it sparked — brought the chamber to a standstill.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal judge calls Trump's deportation of Maryland man 'wholly lawless' in scathing decision
item.description: A federal judge, in a scathing decision on Sunday, said the Trump administration had no legal grounds to arrest, detain, and deport a Salvadoran national living in Maryland to a prison in his home country, saying the decision was “wholly lawless.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, KRON4

item.title: Joe Lake, co-founder of Children's Miracle Network Hospitals, dies at 82
item.description: Joe Lake, co-founder of Children's Miracle Network Hospitals, has passed away, according to the organization. He was 82.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: California town reports third fatality related to rare virus linked to death of Gene Hackman's wife
item.description: Investigators recently blamed the rare virus for the death of Betsy Arakawa, the wife of actor Gene Hackman.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Poor air quality increases depression risk, study finds
item.description: A new study finds poor air quality is linked to a heightened risk for depression. Depression is a classified as a mood disorder.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, KRON4

item.title: GOP senators back crackdown on foreign students
item.description: Republican senators are brushing off concerns that high-profile arrests and deportations of foreign students may ostracize a group that is a major contributor to the U.S. economy. International students injected $43.8 billion into the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Storms tore up two of America's most iconic trails. Federal cuts have disrupted repairs
item.description: CAMPO, Calif. (AP) — Hiking the Pacific Crest Trail is a challenge, especially for adventurers making the entire run from Southern California to Canada, and Eric Kipperman’s job is to greet them at the start and lay bare the difficulties ahead.
item.dc:creator: JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rubio says US is revoking all visas held by South Sudanese passport holders
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Saturday that the United States was revoking all visas held by South Sudanese passport holders, accusing the African nation's government of “taking advantage of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Al Green says he’ll present articles of impeachment against Trump in next 30 days
item.description: Green, who vowed to bring articles of impeachment against Trump roughly two months ago, argued that Trump does not “deserve the office” he holds.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Disgraced former Cardinal Theodore McCarrick has died at age 94
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Theodore McCarrick, a once-powerful Catholic cardinal who was defrocked by Pope Francis in 2019 after a Vatican investigation determined he had molested adults and children, has died. He was 94.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: No more cheap skirts: Trump ends tax exemption for low-value Chinese imports
item.description: A notice to customers dazzled by the low-priced products on Chinese shopping apps: the days of getting trendy clothing, tools and gag gifts that cost less than lunch delivered to your door in 10 days are probably numbered.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, ABC 4
